name: Normalize denormalized data into a proper schema
prompt: Import legacy_data.csv into SQLite and normalize it into a proper relational schema in company.db. Create these tables: (1) departments (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT UNIQUE, budget REAL), (2) employees (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, department_id INTEGER REFERENCES departments(id)), (3) projects (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT UNIQUE, deadline TEXT), (4) assignments (employee_id INTEGER, project_id INTEGER, hours_worked INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY(employee_id, project_id), FOREIGN KEY referencing employees and projects). Populate all tables from the CSV data. Then run queries and save results to migration_report.json: (1) total_employees: count of unique employees, (2) total_projects: count of unique projects, (3) dept_headcount: object mapping department name to employee count, (4) project_hours: array of objects with project_name and total_hours sorted by total_hours descending, (5) top_contributor: the employee name with the most total hours across all projects, (6) engineering_projects: array of unique project names that have at least one Engineering department employee assigned, sorted alphabetically.

criteria.testPattern: departments table|employees table|projects table|assignments table|foreign key

origin.notes: auto-converted from legacy skill-bench TaskDefinition by tools/convert-from-skill-bench.ts
